summary: Surah Al-Ahzab discusses the Battle of the Confederates (Ahzab), the importance of obeying Allah and His Messenger, and the unique status and responsibilities of the Prophet and his family. It outlines social laws concerning adoption, marriage, modesty, and respect for the Prophet. The surah warns against hypocrisy and slander, stresses the consequences of disbelief, and emphasizes the need for sincerity, patience, and remembrance of Allah.
